chunks.title: Passing Arrays to Props.mp3

text: Next thing we are going to learn is how to pass string values in props how to pass numbers Now let's see how to pass items in a collective group in which we have major arrays So if we have to pass an array in a prop For example, we have to define some features for our product Like here we have a product in which we will remove the default of price for now We want to have some features defined in the product So in product tab what we can do is we will pass 1 in price What we can do is in product tab I can create an array called features and in features we can define the features of a product In features we can write that this is a high tech product we can write durable we can write fast So like this we have an array of 3 features which we have defined Now if we have to pass this features array For that we will name our array features is equal to and then in curly braces we will write our array So in curly braces we are going to pass on our features array If we want we can name this array differently We can name it options and here we can pass the options So like this there will be no difference but if you want you can give the same name Let's save this and here what we will do is first we will lock console.log and try to print our features Let's save it and here we will inspect So in console window we have an array printed and in this array we have high tech, durable, fast all these things are available So basically when we passed our array So this is how we will pass our array in curly braces and when we will try to access and print it in product So for now we can make a paragraph and in paragraph we can try to print our features array in curly braces Save and refresh So for now what is written here High tech, durable, fast and one thing you will notice is generally if we send something on a normal HTML page or if we send something from JavaScript and get it printed So it doesn't get printed like this Generally when things are printed then it gets printed on a comma separated array on our page but here there is no comma So simply what is happening is when any component is rendered in JSX So in that if there is any array or object in any prop So what happens is individual item is extracted and then it is printed So high tech, the item on 0th index is added in paragraph and then the second item is picked and then it is added in paragraph i.e. durable is added then the third item is picked and then it is added in paragraph So in this way when we use our props in JSX and they get it printed directly So one individual item is picked and one individual item is sticked So you will not get comma separated values if you will print directly any array Along with that we can see something similar in objects So for objects let's call it options2 In options2 we have high tech in A In B we have something we can keep same values and in C we have fast Let's save it So here we can call it features2 is equal to Now when we pass objects here we will write in curly braces and we can write name of our object So this is our options2 Now to print options2 we can simply do this We will make next paragraph in which we will print features2 and we will write here features2 By the way, these examples we are doing are very basic examples Practically when we use props or when we use other things then we get little extra layer of complexity But because we are in beginner stage and this is our second lecture in which we are learning things So there is nothing to worry if we are taking extra time to learn some concepts because we are trying to learn something new So here our object we will not be able to render it directly but we can render individual key For example, I can access the individual key and we can render it If we want we can change our key and we can render second key So in case of objects, we don't pick individual item Individual item is picked in case of arrays Here we can access it through keys Now this is the way how we are passing our object So we defined it in a different variable and then we passed it But here there is another way which you will see frequently which is that we are directly passing our features here For example, this options array instead of this we will write our internal array by putting square brackets Here we will pass high tech and let's pass durable and let's save it and refresh So now we have high tech and durable So in this way, it is possible to pass an array directly and in the same way, we can pass an object directly and when we pass an object we have to notice one thing that we write another curly braces for our object and here we can write A is going to be high tech like this and here we have to notice that in features this is our syntax and in
